skill.name: zach-product-research
skill.description: Run Sorftime-based Amazon product research to evaluate market demand, competition, customer pain points, and Go/No-Go decisions.
skill.summary: Analyze Amazon product opportunities with Sorftime data, structured reports, dashboards, and decision scorecards.
skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: The high static score is partly inflated by Markdown backticks, template literals, CSS repeat syntax, and escaped dashboard rendering. However, AI review confirmed unsafe filesystem writes from unsanitized payload paths and raw legacy HTML passthrough, so this community skill is high risk and not safe to publish without fixes. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

high_findings.title: Unsanitized Output Paths Allow File Write Outside Intended Directory
high_findings.description: The renderer builds output directories from payload metadata and writes artifact filenames directly under that directory. Metadata fields and artifact names are not sanitized, so an untrusted payload can use traversal segments or crafted names to write outside the expected report folder.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code joins untrusted metadata and artifact filenames into Path objects and writes files without canonical path containment checks. This is a direct file write risk when payloads are not trusted.

high_findings.title: Legacy HTML Payload Is Written Without Sanitization
high_findings.description: In v1 compatibility mode, the renderer writes report_html from the payload directly to an HTML file. If a payload contains script content, opening the generated report can execute attacker-controlled browser code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sink writes payload HTML verbatim. Exploitation requires a malicious or compromised payload, but the unsafe behavior is explicit.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local Tool Permissions and Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill manifest allows Bash, Write, and Edit, and the workflow instructs users to run local Python render and validation scripts. This is legitimate for report generation, but it increases impact if prompts or payloads are untrusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permissions and script execution instructions are clearly present. They are part of the intended workflow, so the issue is elevated operational risk rather than proof of malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Local MCP Configuration May Contain Secrets
medium_findings.description: The instructions mention a local Sorftime MCP configuration file in the user home directory and warn not to reveal the real key. This reduces risk, but it still directs the assistant toward a file that may contain credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly references a hidden local MCP config and keys, but it also includes a warning not to disclose them. Human review is appropriate before approving marketplace publication.

medium_findings.title: User-Provided Regex Rules Can Cause Resource Exhaustion
medium_findings.description: The Top100 parser applies regex patterns loaded from a rules file to product titles. Malicious or poorly designed patterns can cause excessive CPU use on large product lists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The regex patterns are data-driven and applied repeatedly. This is a plausible ReDoS risk, although normal trusted rule files make it less severe.

low_findings.title: Dashboard innerHTML Findings Are Mostly Escaped Template Rendering
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged innerHTML in the dashboard template. The template uses escapeHtml before inserting dynamic text in the inspected render paths, so these hits are mostly false positives with low residual maintenance risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The escaping helper is defined and used at the flagged sinks reviewed. The residual concern is future edits that could bypass the helper.

low_findings.title: Many Static External Command Hits Are Markdown or Template Syntax
low_findings.description: Several static findings are inline code examples, documentation paths, JavaScript template literals, or CSS repeat syntax rather than Ruby backtick execution or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation bullets, Markdown code formatting, or CSS layout syntax. They do not execute commands by themselves.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsanitized Output Paths Allow File Write Outside Intended Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The renderer builds output directories from payload metadata and writes artifact filenames directly under that directory. Metadata fields and artifact names are not sanitized, so an untrusted payload can use traversal segments or crafted names to write outside the expected report folder.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code joins untrusted metadata and artifact filenames into Path objects and writes files without canonical path containment checks. This is a direct file write risk when payloads are not trusted.

dangerous_patterns.title: Legacy HTML Payload Is Written Without Sanitization
dangerous_patterns.description: In v1 compatibility mode, the renderer writes report_html from the payload directly to an HTML file. If a payload contains script content, opening the generated report can execute attacker-controlled browser code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sink writes payload HTML verbatim. Exploitation requires a malicious or compromised payload, but the unsafe behavior is explicit.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Provided Regex Rules Can Cause Resource Exhaustion
dangerous_patterns.description: The Top100 parser applies regex patterns loaded from a rules file to product titles. Malicious or poorly designed patterns can cause excessive CPU use on large product lists.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The regex patterns are data-driven and applied repeatedly. This is a plausible ReDoS risk, although normal trusted rule files make it less severe.

content.value_statement: Amazon sellers need reliable market research before they invest in a product. This skill uses Sorftime data and structured analysis to produce reports, dashboards, Excel sheets, and Go/No-Go decisions.
actual_capabilities: Guides Sorftime MCP data collection for categories, keywords, products, reviews, and 1688 supply references.
Structures product research into market overview, dimension analysis, cross analysis, competitor review, VOC, and decision chapters.
Uses helper scripts to render Markdown, HTML, dashboard, Excel, and JSON deliverables from a unified payload.
Provides validation checks for required sheets, chapter completeness, and report quality gates.
Supports Top100 title parsing and cross-dimension opportunity gap analysis from local JSON inputs.
Produces Go/No-Go scorecards and tiered product strategy recommendations.
limitations: Requires Sorftime MCP access for the primary data workflow.
Generated conclusions depend on the quality and freshness of the Sorftime data and user-provided payloads.
Local scripts write files to disk and should not be run on untrusted payloads without review.
The skill does not replace legal, financial, or supply chain due diligence.

use_cases.title: Validate a New Product Idea
use_cases.description: Assess demand, competition, reviews, price bands, and entry barriers before sourcing inventory.

use_cases.title: Find Category Gaps
use_cases.description: Parse Top100 products and compare dimensions to find underserved product combinations.

use_cases.title: Prepare Investor-Ready Research
use_cases.description: Create a structured research package with Markdown, HTML, dashboard, Excel, and decision evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Market Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use zach-product-research to analyze the US Amazon market for laptop backpacks. Focus on demand, competition, price range, and a clear Go/No-Go recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Category Opportunity Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use zach-product-research to compare three related Amazon categories in the US market. Identify the best category for a small brand with a moderate launch budget.

prompt_templates.title: Competitor and VOC Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use zach-product-research to study competitors and negative reviews for portable monitors. Map pain points to product features and pricing recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Full Deliverable Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use zach-product-research to run a full Sorftime research workflow for pet water fountains. Produce the unified payload, validate it, and generate all report deliverables.

output_examples.input: Analyze the US market for magnetic power banks.
output_examples.output: A market research package with an executive summary, KPI tables, competitor logic, VOC pain points, opportunity gaps, and a Go/No-Go scorecard.

output_examples.input: Find underserved combinations in standing desk accessories.
output_examples.output: A cross-dimension analysis showing high demand and low supply segments, with reasons, priority scores, and product strategy suggestions.

output_examples.input: Prepare final deliverables for a selected Amazon category.
output_examples.output: Markdown, HTML, dashboard, Excel, and supporting data files generated from the same structured research payload.

best_practices: Review the unified payload before running local generation scripts.
Confirm Sorftime data dates and marketplace region before using the final recommendation.
Keep raw data, intermediate files, and final reports together for auditability.
anti_patterns: Do not run the renderer on payloads from unknown sources without checking paths and HTML fields.
Do not treat a Go/No-Go score as a final sourcing decision without margin and supplier validation.
Do not publish reports that contain unsupported numbers or missing Excel source sheets.

faq.question: Does this skill require Sorftime MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. The main workflow depends on Sorftime MCP tools for category, keyword, product, review, and supply data.

faq.question: What deliverables can it create?
faq.answer: It can create Markdown reports, HTML summaries, dashboards, Excel workbooks, and supporting JSON files.

faq.question: Can I use my own market data?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide structured payloads, but you should verify paths and content before running scripts.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to run automatically?
faq.answer: No. The audit found unsafe file path and raw HTML handling. Review inputs before executing scripts.

faq.question: Does it make final business decisions?
faq.answer: No. It provides evidence and recommendations. Users still need supplier, margin, compliance, and launch validation.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
